What is the Income Tax Act 2025 and when: The Income Tax Act 2025 came into effect from 1st April 2025. It is the latest version of the Income Tax Act and is applicable for the financial year 2025-26. The Act provides a comprehensive framework for taxation of individuals, companies, and other entities in India. It includes provisions for income tax, surcharge, cess, deductions, exemptions, and other relevant details. The Act also outlines the procedures for filing returns, claiming deductions, and making payments. The Act is subject to change from time to time, and the latest version is available on the official website of the Income Tax Department.

Does the Income Tax Act 2025 change my t: The Income Tax Act 2025 does not change the tax rates or liability. It only provides for the new tax regime for FY 2026-27 and onwards. The old tax regime will continue to apply for FY 2025-26 and onwards.

What happens to pending income tax proce: If the taxpayer is not satisfied with the decision of the Income Tax Officer, he can file a revision petition before the Income Tax Appellate Tribunal. How does the Tax Year concept affect adv: Advance tax is calculated on the basis of the Tax Year. The Tax Year is the period from 1st April to 31st March. Advance tax is payable on income earned in the previous year. What is faceless assessment under Income: Faceless assessment is a new feature introduced in the Income Tax Act 2025. It is a new feature that allows taxpayers to file their return of income online without providing their personal details. This feature is available for taxpayers who have income from business or profession, and who have filed their return of income online in the previous financial year.
